title: Bridge Essay: Aphantasia: The science of visual imagery extremes.
concept: Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**Aphantasia: The science of visual imagery extremes.** — Handb Clin Neurol (2021). DOI: 10.1016/B978-0-12-821377-3.00012-X. PMID: 33832681. Visual imagery allows us to revisit the appearance of things in their absence and to test out virtual combinations of sensory experience. Visual imagery has been linked to many cognitive processes, such as autobiographical and visual working memory. Imagery also plays symptomatic and mechanistic roles in neurologic and mental disorders and is utilized in treatment.
